summary: Route the geocoding subcommand to geocoding-api.open-meteo.com instead of api.open-meteo.com to fix HTTP 404.
reason: Reported in mvanhorn/printing-press-library#502. Open-Meteo splits its API across two hosts: api.open-meteo.com for forecast/air-quality and geocoding-api.open-meteo.com for geocoding search. The spec uses the forecast host as base_url, and the generated geocoding promoted command routed /search through that host, returning HTTP 404. The working internal helper geocodeLookup in location.go already calls the correct host directly; this patch mirrors that approach in promoted_geocoding.go, bypassing the spec-derived client for this one endpoint. Verified live: geocoding Chandigarh returns lat=30.73629, lon=76.7884 matching the issue reporter is curl evidence.
